chunks.text: Congratulations to Farhana Sheikh, Newly Named IEEE Fellow | Berkeley Wireless Research Center Skip to main content Congratulations to Farhana Sheikh, Newly Named IEEE Fellow December 5, 2025 Farhana Sheikh has been named an IEEE Fellow, effective Ja

Sheikh has been named an IEEE Fellow, effective January 1, 2026, in recognition of her contributions to digital signal processing and 2.5D/3D heterogeneous integration. The grade of Fellow is conferred upon IEEE Senior Members with outstanding record

d upon IEEE Senior Members with outstanding records of accomplishments in any of the IEEE fields of interest. Each year, this honor is limited to no more than 0.1% of the total IEEE voting membership. Fellowship is the highest grade of IEEE membershi

Fellowship is the highest grade of IEEE membership and is regarded as a prestigious honor and a significant career achievement. The IEEE is the world’s leading professional organization dedicated to advancing technology for the benefit of humanity.

advancing technology for the benefit of humanity. With over 500,000 members in 190 countries, IEEE is a global authority across electrical and computer sciences, engineering, and related disciplines. IEEE publishes approximately one-third of the worl

IEEE publishes approximately one-third of the world’s technical literature in electrical engineering, computing, and electronics, maintains an active portfolio of over 1,000 standards, and sponsors more than 2,000 conferences annually. For more infor

re than 2,000 conferences annually. For more information about IEEE, please visit the IEEE website. We are delighted to share this news about a distinguished alumnus of the Berkeley Wireless Research Center.
